programmes.show_description: OJ Borg is live through the night from the Salford Riviera with Midnight Mastermind, Wish You Were There, Midnight Caller and a huge Ravio 2 to end the week.

programmes.show_name: Dance Sounds of the 90s with Vernon Kay
programmes.show_description: Vernon Kay takes you on a nostalgic trip back to the 90s dance floor with music and memories to get you moving.
Welcome! It's Early Breakfast with Owain Wyn Evans, featuring Airdrum Anthems and Step Back in Time. It's your weekend warm up! Owain asks what you cant wait for this weekend!
It's Big Guest Friday with Claire Foy, Jane McDonald and Les Dennis in the studio! Plus, the Wonder Years start in 1993.
Comedian and writer Bob Mortimer selects his final two Tracks Of My Years of the week and two listeners take on our quiz, Ten To The Top.
Jeremy and guests discuss the news headlines and talk to the people making them.
Trevor is bringing you some of the best music from across the decades to get you through the afternoon.
It's All Request Friday, and Sara wants to hear from you on 88291 or sara.cox@bbc.co.uk.
You've found the place where you can boogie tonight... Michelle's Fabulous Friday party!
Michelle's Mood is...DIANA ROSS! 30 minutes of non-stop bops from The Boss.
There's party tunes all the way and with the 2026 MOBO Awards coming up, Sophie plays some of her favourite winners from down the years in the Disco Wonderland.

programmes.show_name: The Rock Show with Shaun Keaveny
programmes.show_description: Shaun salutes Motörhead guitarist Phil Campbell, who died last week, with another chance to hear Phil's Rock God from 2019.

programmes.show_name: Romesh Ranganathan: For the Love of Hip Hop
programmes.show_description: 50 Cent joins Romesh to talk about the legacy of Get Rich Or Die Tryin' on its 20th anniversary. Plus, tracks from Jurassic 5, A Tribe Called Quest and Jay-Z.
Tony is back with more fabulous Soul music celebrating 50 years of George Benson's Breezin', and birthdays for Chaka Khan, Stephanie Mills, Diana Ross and Aretha Franklin.
Vernon Kay presents highlights of the performances from this year's Piano Room Month, accompanied by the BBC Concert Orchestra in the BBC's Maida Vale studios.
Level 42 performing live at Hammersmith Odeon in 1985.  Featuring The Chinese Way, Leaving Me Now, The Chant Has Begun, Love Games, Turn It On, Micro Kid and How Can It Be.

programmes.show_name: The Unwinds Mix with Angela Griffin
programmes.show_description: Angela Griffin's Pop Unwinds Mix with Wham, Abba, Take That and Spice Girls.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 60s with Tony Blackburn
programmes.show_description: Tony brings you two hours of music from the best decade ever, with hits from Engelbert Humperdinck, Helen Shapiro and The Kinks. This week, the top three comes from 1967.
Joel Dommett sits in and chats to Ed Gamble about his new upcoming tour Fresh Hell. Plus, The Mystery Voice and Bon Jovi vinyl.
Romesh brings the laughs, soundtracked by great music. As well as chatting to brilliant guests, he's sharing your hilarious stories, so you can start your Saturday with a smile!
Spend Saturday lunchtime with Emma and enjoy a menu full of your favourite music, great conversation and a delicious dive into the BBC archives for dessert.
Rylan is joined by Corinne Bailey Rae for a very special KaRYoke to celebrate 20 years since the release of her hit single Put Your Records On.
Shaun Keaveny with fabulous teatime tunes and your tales of the extraordinary and the everyday.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 80s with Gary Davies
programmes.show_description: Thomas Dolby shares his favourite 80s Moments, and there's a Pet Shop Boys 1986 Mastermix.

programmes.show_description: Join Fearne for a nostalgia drenched celebration of the best 90s music and pop culture.
